title: Create hello.py that prints a fixed greeting
description: Create a file `hello.py` in the workspace root. When run with `python3 hello.py`, it must print exactly `hello from the harness` followed by a newline. No other output, no command-line arguments.
acceptance_criteria: File hello.py exists at the workspace root.
Running `python3 hello.py` prints `hello from the harness` followed by a newline, with no extra output and exit code 0.

title: Scaffold the todo_cli package with an empty entrypoint
description: Create a `todo_cli/` package with `__init__.py` and `__main__.py`. `__main__.py` should define a `main()` function that prints `usage: todo [add|list|done|remove]` to stderr and exits 2 when called with no args. Running `python3 -m todo_cli` should invoke main().
acceptance_criteria: Running `python3 -m todo_cli` prints the usage line to stderr and exits with code 2.

title: Implement the `add` subcommand
description: Extend `todo_cli` so that `python3 -m todo_cli add <text>` appends a new open item to `TODOS.md` in the current working directory and exits 0. Multiple words after `add` are joined with single spaces. The on-disk format is one item per line: `- [ ] <text>` for open and `- [x] <text>` for done. Adding when TODOS.md does not exist creates it; adding when it exists preserves prior content. With no text after `add`, print a usage line to stderr and exit 2.
acceptance_criteria: Running `python3 -m todo_cli add buy milk` in an empty cwd creates TODOS.md whose contents are exactly `- [ ] buy milk\n` and exits 0.
Running `python3 -m todo_cli add` with no further arguments prints a usage line to stderr and exits 2.
Adding to a non-empty TODOS.md appends the new open item without modifying or reordering prior lines.

title: Implement the `list` subcommand
description: Extend `todo_cli` so that `python3 -m todo_cli list` reads `TODOS.md` from the current working directory and prints each item to stdout, one per line, with a 1-based index in the form `N. [ ] text` or `N. [x] text`, preserving the order in the file. When TODOS.md is missing or contains no valid item lines, print exactly `(no todos)` followed by a newline and exit 0. Lines that don't match the `- [ ] ` / `- [x] ` prefix are ignored on read.
acceptance_criteria: With a TODOS.md containing two items, `list` prints them with 1-based indices in original order and exits 0.
With TODOS.md missing, `list` prints exactly `(no todos)` followed by a newline to stdout and exits 0.
With TODOS.md present but containing no lines matching the item format, `list` prints exactly `(no todos)` and exits 0.

title: Implement the `done` subcommand
description: Extend `todo_cli` so that `python3 -m todo_cli done <index>` marks the 1-based item at <index> in `TODOS.md` as done by flipping `- [ ]` to `- [x]`. The operation is idempotent: marking an already-done item leaves the file unchanged and exits 0. Other items must not be reordered or modified. With no index, a non-integer index, or an index out of range (including any index when TODOS.md is missing), print a usage line to stderr and exit 2.
acceptance_criteria: With TODOS.md containing two open items, `done 1` flips the first to `- [x]` and leaves the second untouched.
Running `done <n>` on an item already marked done leaves the file unchanged and exits 0.
Running `done` with no index, with a non-integer, or with an out-of-range index prints to stderr and exits 2.
